summary: Sprint 5 'Local Market & Safety Net' full backend + frontend testing. All 4 new features tested: Rescue Mode, Location Detection, LocalMarketPulseWidget, Viability Badge. 25 backend tests all passed. Frontend working after Metro cache clear.

ui_bugs.issue: IP geolocation fallback (https://ipapi.co/json/) fails with CORS policy error when called from browser. After GPS permission fails (expected in web), the IP fallback also fails silently. No user feedback shown on failure - button stays in original state. Users cannot auto-detect location on web.
ui_bugs.rca: ipapi.co blocks CORS requests from the production domain. Fix: proxy the IP geolocation through backend endpoint, or use a CORS-friendly alternative like https://ipinfo.io/json or https://ip-api.com/json

ui_bugs.issue: Console warning: 'Unexpected text node: A text node cannot be a child of a View' - fires repeatedly when stepping through questionnaire. Likely caused by whitespace/ternary structure around location step rendering. Non-blocking but pollutes console.

issues: No user feedback when location detection fails - the button doesn't show an error message, users see no indication of failure

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix IP geolocation CORS issue in questionnaire step 5. Move ipapi.co call to a backend proxy endpoint (e.g., GET /api/location/detect) to avoid CORS. Alternatively, use POST to https://ipinfo.io/json which supports CORS better.
LOW: Add error feedback in location step when detection fails - show 'Could not auto-detect, please skip' message
LOW: Fix 'Unexpected text node' React Native warning in questionnaire.tsx (whitespace around ternary rendering of location step)
critical_code_review_comments: CORS issue: questionnaire.tsx line 109 calls 'fetch(https://ipapi.co/json/)' directly from the client. This is blocked by CORS in production browser environments. The ipapi.co service requires either a paid plan for CORS support or the call must be proxied through the backend.
server.py is 1739 lines - approaching the 700-line limit for splitting. Consider modularizing Sprint 5 routes (rescue, location, viability) into separate router files.

Rescue mode: POST /api/rescue/{user_id}/{blueprint_id} path parameter uses 'blueprint_id' but the route looks up the ID in the 'ideas' collection, not blueprints. Consider renaming to 'idea_id' for clarity.
LocalMarketPulseWidget: The 'View all' button in the widget doesn't navigate anywhere (no onPress handler observed) - may be placeholder.

success_rate.frontend: 85% - all Sprint 5 features visible and functional except IP geolocation fallback fails due to CORS

test_credentials.stuck_idea_setup: Both users have saved_idea for gig-001 with last_activity=100 hours ago (stuck=True)

seed_data_creation: Created TEST_sprint5_architect@blueprint.com user. Set is_architect=True on 73783175-017f-41f9-8fc9-3bcc8dfbcf55. Created stuck saved_ideas for both test users with gig-001 idea (last activity 100h ago).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Sprint 5 all features work except location IP geolocation (CORS). Metro cache was cleared during this test run. Test users: TEST_sprint5_architect@blueprint.com (architect+Austin city) and TEST_sprint4_tester@blueprint.com (non-architect). Both have stuck saved_ideas for gig-001. Backend tests in /app/backend/tests/test_sprint5.py (25 tests, all pass). Key env note: Metro cache at /app/frontend/.metro-cache/cache was cleared - if stale bundle issues recur, clear and restart expo.

ipapi_cors_failure.root_cause: ipapi.co/json endpoint does not include Access-Control-Allow-Origin header for the production domain. Client-side fetch is blocked by browser CORS policy.
ipapi_cors_failure.reproduction: Go to questionnaire step 5, click 'Unlock Local Market Data', observe console error: 'Access to fetch at https://ipapi.co/json/ from origin http://localhost:8081 has been blocked by CORS policy'
ipapi_cors_failure.mitigation: Add backend proxy endpoint GET /api/location/ip-detect that calls ipapi.co server-side and returns city/country. Frontend calls the proxy instead of ipapi.co directly.

metro_bundle_cache.root_cause: Stale Metro bundle cached old version of home.tsx without LocalMarketPulseWidget import. After clearing /app/frontend/.metro-cache/cache and restarting expo supervisor, widget rendered correctly.
metro_bundle_cache.note: Production users would always get the latest bundle, so this is a dev environment issue only.
